_comment: NPC: Qing Shui (清水). Canon ID: N30. Per user directive: every miscellaneous NPC from RI must be accounted for. Prime Directive: Reality is objective; cultivation changes understanding, not existence. Every being described here exists independently of the player's perception.
name: Qing Shui
relationship_to_wanglin: mentor; gave Wang Lin the Slaughter Sword

perception_tiers.nascent_soul: Third Step; water dao; Wang Lin's mentor

note: Gave Wang Lin the Slaughter Sword; mentor figure

personality_traits._comment: NOVEL_STATEMENT: serene water-aspected mentor. High patience, high honor, high dao_heart. Low greed, low ambition (already Third Step). Moderately curious about promising disciples. Per Article XLI: these traits apply to ANY serene mentor figure.

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi. Per canon: Wang Lin's Dao heart is famously tested at every major realm.

initiation_lines: The water does not resist. It does not fight. It simply flows around the obstacle and continues. This is the Dao I teach. Most disciples do not understand it for decades.
Wang Lin understood it in hours. That is why I gave him the Slaughter Sword. Not because he was strong. Because he understood that strength and water are not opposites.
The Corpse Yin Sect cultivates death. The Soul Refining Sect cultivates souls. I cultivate understanding. All three paths lead to the same mountain. The view is different from each.
Books and experience. If you wish to learn, bring these. I will teach you what water has taught me.

daily_schedule.action: Deep meditation by the spirit spring

sect_tasks.name: Dao Instruction Materials
sect_tasks.description: Qing Shui needs books and experience bottles to prepare Dao instruction for promising disciples.

sect_tasks.name: Spirit Spring Tending
sect_tasks.description: The spirit spring requires coal and glowstone to maintain its spiritual purity. Qing Shui entrusts this to capable disciples.
